learning_outcomes.description: Apply basic oral epidemiology terminology, definitions and calculations using manual and technology-assisted methods
Critically review and synthesise existing evidence from a range of sources to develop coherent and logical arguments
Produce informed and original research questions and hypotheses for a health-based context
Compare and contrast study designs used to answer clinical questions in oral health research
Interpret and professionally communicate evidence-based practice knowledge, skills and ideas to expert audiences

assessments.task_description:
This is an in-semester task that involves a tutorial problem set covering applied concepts from tutorials between week 1 and week 10 and may include dental research designs in dental public health.

The details of the task description and marking criteria will be made available on the DENT3021 Blackboard course site.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
In this assessment, you will develop research skills that are essential for applying an evidence-based approach to your clinical practice. You will also improve your formal academic research writing skills, including structuring your work effectively, ensuring logical progression and flow, organizing paragraphs, and using appropriate citation styles. These skills will be crucial as you prepare for your final year research project. The assessment will involve finding relevant literature related to an oral health research question and then methodically analysing this literature to assess the information and evidence used to support the research.

The details of the task description and marking criteria will be made available on the DENT3021 Blackboard course site.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: A 90 minute written exam that may consist of multiple choice, short answer questions, short essays and problem-solving covering content from Week 1 to Week 13 (inclusive).
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
